steps.title: Factor out the GCF
steps.content:
Each term in $2x^2+38x+10$ is divisible by $2$:
$$2x^2+38x+10=2(x^2+19x+5)$$

steps.title: Check that the remaining quadratic is irreducible over integers
steps.content: For $x^2+19x+5$, we need two integers whose product is $5$ and sum is $19$. The only integer pairs giving product $5$ are $(1,5)$ and $(-1,-5)$, neither summing to $19$. So $2$ is the only clean factor among the listed choices.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• A) $2$: divides every term, so it is a factor. Keep.
  • B) $5x$: $2x^2/5x$ is not a polynomial, so $5x$ is not a factor. Eliminate.
  • C) $38x$: $10/38x$ is not a polynomial, so $38x$ is not a factor. Eliminate.
  • D) $2x^2$: $38x/2x^2$ is not a polynomial, so $2x^2$ is not a factor. Eliminate.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
